blocks.content: Julian heard the phone call through the wall.
Not the words — just the tenor. One voice that carried, the flat tone of a speaker. Then silence. Then the quality of five people processing something at the same time.
He was at the kitchen table with his legal pad. He'd been up since five. He'd filled three pages.
He looked at what he'd written.
The locked door is the argument they're all making. The argument that almost works. Door locked from inside, window impossible, therefore nobody got in. Therefore natural.
But natural causes don't open windows on cold nights. Natural causes don't explain someone tracking a man at a bonfire with the patience of someone who's already decided.
He wrote a single line at the top of a clean page.
The door was never the way in.
He stared at it. Underlined it.
His statement at the station was at ten o'clock. He had two hours. He was going to need them.
